title: R stylo Worker and Computational Parity
objective: Run versioned whole-text stylometric calculations through a fixed R stylo worker, validate every scientific output before lifecycle success, and establish fixture-local computational parity and known-only fitting invariance without claiming preprocessing, benchmark, or production validation.
scope: Closed versioned input, output, and error contracts for whole-text feature-count analysis
Fixed Rscript entrypoint and Python adapter with no shell interpolation or user-controlled argv, environment, or path syntax
A validated scientific-finalization phase that separates process exit from accepted analysis outcome
Known-only MFW ranking, culling, scaling, and feature inventory with unknown projection after fitting
Classic Burrows Delta as the primary distance and Eder's Delta plus Cosine Delta as separate sensitivity distances
Finite-number, non-negative distance, matrix shape, symmetry, diagonal, label, cardinality, and cell-outcome validation
Deterministic project-authored CC0 fixture proposals and an independent direct-stylo reference harness
Canonical Linux environment evidence including R, stylo, package, locale, timezone, RNG, BLAS, LAPACK, and protocol versions
Parity, leakage-canary, injection, malformed-output, timeout, cancellation, partial-failure, clean-clone, and CI evidence
non_goals: Raw-text tokenization, Unicode normalization, punctuation or number removal, segmentation, duplicate detection, or corpus-health validation, which belong to P007
Public Upload-to-Run activation, parameter wizard completion, or browser end-to-end analysis, which belong to P008
Interpretation cards, result graphics, stability labels, or open-set behavior, which belong to P009 and P011
Known-author accuracy, diachronic benchmark performance, threshold calibration, or complete leakage validation, which belong to P010 and P011
FAIR-oriented run package, clean-room rerun, or general reproducibility claim, which belongs to P012 and P015
Pinocchio, LiberLiber, or any literary case-study result, which belongs to P013
Production resource numbers, egress denial, container hardening, host isolation, or deployment, which belongs to P014
A new Delta formula, replacement of stylo, general stylo parity, CE-04 completion before P007, or CE-07 completion before P010 and P011

acceptance.criterion: Versioned input, output, and error schemas plus canonical Python models, duplicate-key and non-finite rejection, and semantic validators pass positive and adversarial contract fixtures.

The adapter uses a fixed Rscript --vanilla entrypoint, shell false, fixed trusted files and cwd, a minimal allowlisted environment, private output creation, and no user value in argv, R code, environment names, or filesystem paths; injection fixtures execute no command or code.

Process exit, output capture, schema validation, scientific validation, durable terminal state, and guardian acknowledgement occur in that order; exit zero with missing, malformed, non-finite, asymmetric, mislabeled, duplicate, or cardinality-invalid output never becomes success.
MFW ranking, culling, means, standard deviations, and selected features use known documents only; changing an unknown canary leaves all fitting artifacts and known-known distances exactly unchanged.

Every requested cell appears exactly once; not-enough-features remains explicit, complete, partial, and failed analysis outcomes are derived deterministically, and omitted or corrupt cells invalidate the envelope rather than being projected as completion.

For the predeclared whole-text synthetic fixture suite and locked R/stylo environment, ordered feature inventories match the independent direct-stylo oracle exactly, distance matrices differ by at most 1e-6, and nearest-neighbor tie groups match at the predeclared threshold.

Canonical Linux runs retain fixture and input hashes, direct-reference and worker outputs, parity and leakage reports, complete session information, package and image identity, locale and timezone, and all expected failed cells without private paths or research payload leakage.

Exact-commit clean-clone and Linux CI gates pass with full measured source coverage; a separate HumanDecision accepts the fixture, environment, tolerance, and claim protocol before oracle outputs are frozen, while public analysis and P007-P015 claims remain explicitly locked.

changed_files: P006 execution brief, native PromptEvent, method/security architecture audit, proposed parity ADR, active Ticket, checkpoint, and synchronized startup pointers
Deterministic pre-sample process-completion regression coverage added after the repeated opening gate exposed timing-dependent coverage
Accepted HumanDecision and ADR update for the CC0 fixture, locked environment, tolerance, metric, known-only fitting, and fixture-local claim protocol
Closed v1 worker input, result, and fatal-error models, generated schemas, strict single-parse JSON boundary, scientific semantic validator, and pure finalizer
Bounded nonblocking private workspace output read with pre-open type checks, identity fingerprints, mutation detection, and current-path area rebinding
Adversarial method and security regressions plus synchronized project memory, handoff, start instructions, checkpoint, and retained validation evidence
Premature lifecycle and guardian ACK integration removed after independent review; P006-AC-03 remains pending
Exact contracts/finalizer implementation commit passed Linux verification, SBOM/dependency audit, and canonical Linux amd64 container gates; only P006-AC-01 and P006-AC-05 pass at this checkpoint
Project-authored CC0 complete, unknown-canary, and culling-boundary fixtures plus an independent direct-stylo oracle, separate schema, generator, validators, and locked canonical environment

Two byte-identical no-network Linux oracle runs retained with source, image identity, observed package versions, lockfile, session, input, output, and checksum binding; one-time write workflow removed after freeze

Post-freeze method audit retained v1 as an insufficient intermediate oracle because equal token totals and one final-position unknown cannot detect raw-count or positional-role worker errors; strengthened v2 required before comparison

commands.result: The P006 opening package passed 970 tests, 100% of 6,551 statements and 1,732 branches, strict typing, metadata, 77 provenance records, repository scanning, and the locked R/stylo boundary on macOS.

A repeated opening gate passed all 970 tests but reached 99.96% measured coverage because the pre-sample completion branch depended on real-process timing; the failure is retained and a deterministic regression test was added without changing production source.

The corrected P006 opening package passed 971 tests, 100% of 6,551 statements and 1,732 branches, strict typing, metadata, 77 provenance records, repository scanning, and the locked R/stylo boundary on macOS.
Exact start commit 82cc679 passed Linux source, schema, record, SBOM, dependency-audit, and canonical Linux amd64 container jobs; it establishes opening-package integrity only, not R worker parity.

The first post-audit full gate passed all 1,072 behavior tests but reached 99.89% measured coverage across 7,215 statements and 1,898 branches; the failed gate is retained and seven defensive lines received direct adversarial tests.

The corrected contracts/finalizer checkpoint passed 1,073 tests, 100% of 7,215 statements and 1,898 branches, strict typing, metadata, 79 provenance records, repository scanning, and the locked R boundary on macOS.

After the finite-number and FIFO-race re-audit corrections, the checkpoint passed 1,075 tests, 100% of 7,216 statements and 1,898 branches, strict typing, metadata, 80 provenance records, repository scanning, and the locked R boundary on macOS; the final narrow scientific review returned PASS.

Exact implementation commit 3c6ebe5 passed Linux source, schema, record, measured-coverage, SBOM, dependency-audit, and canonical Linux amd64 container jobs; it establishes the bounded contracts/finalizer checkpoint only, not R worker parity.

commands.command: GitHub Actions oracle freeze runs 29294170144, 29294530963, and 29294900147
commands.result: Three capture attempts stopped before evidence publication and exposed, in order, an over-broad environment error, an unreadable root-owned locked R cache, and public synthetic outputs unreadable by the separate host verifier. Each cause was corrected without accepting or rewriting scientific values.

The corrected oracle source checkpoint passed 1,086 tests, 100% of 7,247 statements and 1,902 branches, strict typing, metadata, 80 provenance records, repository scanning, R parsing, and the locked R environment on macOS.
Exact oracle source commit 7df1fdf passed Linux verification, SBOM/dependency audit, and canonical Linux amd64 container construction before evidence capture.

commands.command: GitHub Actions P006 direct-stylo oracle freeze run 29295419981
commands.result: The locked Linux amd64 oracle ran twice without network access; both four-file output directories were byte-identical, passed input/scientific validation, and were retained in checksum-bound evidence commit b5a842f.
